name: Sinking of Cruiser Moskva
detail: Ukraine's R-360 Neptune anti-ship missiles strike and sink the Russian Black Sea Fleet flagship Moskva on April 13-14, 2022. Largest warship destroyed in combat since 1982. Forces Russia to withdraw fleet from northwestern Black Sea.

name: Kerch Bridge Strike (Oct 2022)
detail: Massive explosion on the Kerch Strait Bridge on Putin's 70th birthday disrupts critical supply line to Crimea. Two road sections collapse; fuel tanker fire on rail section. Russia responds with mass missile strikes on Ukrainian cities.

name: Kharkiv Oblast Counteroffensive
detail: Ukraine's surprise armored offensive in September 2022 liberates over 8,000 sq km in Kharkiv Oblast in days, including the key logistics hub of Izium. Russia's largest defeat since the failed Kyiv offensive. Evidence of torture chambers found in liberated areas.

name: Liberation of Kherson City
detail: Ukraine liberates Kherson city on November 11, 2022 as Russia retreats across the Dnipro River. First occupied regional capital retaken. Zelensky visits the liberated city within 24 hours.

name: Naval Drone Campaign vs Black Sea Fleet
detail: Ukraine's campaign of naval drone attacks on the Black Sea Fleet (2022-2024) damaged or sank multiple Russian warships, ultimately forcing Russia to relocate its fleet from Sevastopol to Novorossiysk. The degraded fleet enabled Ukraine to resume partial Black Sea shipping.

name: Kursk Oblast Incursion
detail: Ukraine's August 2024 cross-border incursion into Russia's Kursk Oblast captured 1,000+ sq km including Sudzha. First foreign military occupation of Russian soil since WWII. Forced Russia to divert forces from Donbas and embarrassed Putin domestically.

name: HIMARS Strikes on Russian Ammunition Depots
detail: From June 2022, Ukraine used US-supplied HIMARS rockets to destroy Russian ammunition depots behind front lines. Over 400 confirmed strikes on high-value targets. Significantly reduced Russian artillery fire rates and disrupted logistics. Russia relocated most large depots beyond HIMARS range (70km).

name: Storm Shadow Strike Sinks Novocherkassk Landing Ship
detail: Ukraine used UK-supplied Storm Shadow cruise missiles to strike and sink the Russian landing ship Novocherkassk in Feodosia harbor, Crimea, on December 26, 2023. The third Russian landing ship sunk or severely damaged in Crimean ports, further degrading Russia's naval amphibious capability.

name: Bayraktar TB2 Drone Campaign (Early War)
detail: Ukraine's Bayraktar TB2 combat drones proved devastatingly effective in the first weeks of the war (Feb-April 2022), destroying hundreds of Russian vehicles, artillery systems, and an air defense radar. TB2's effectiveness forced Russia to strengthen air defense, and the drone became a symbol of Ukrainian resistance globally.
